data.title: Vision Marine posted Q1 revenue of $15.7M
data.description:
• Vision Marine Technologies (VMAR) for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, total revenues were $15.7 million, compared to $0.1 million in the prior-year period, driven primarily by the scale of the company's expanded U.S. retail platform following the inclusion of NVG's dealership operations.
  • Gross profit for the quarter totalled $4.2 million or 27%, reflecting the operating leverage of the expanded retail network.
  • The company generated $1.9 million of cash provided by operating activities, marking its first positive operating cash flow quarter following a period in which operations had historically consumed cash. 
  • As at November 30, 2025, Vision Marine reported cash of $2.3 million and a working capital surplus of about $4.7 million. These figures do not include the company's $9.6 million of gross proceeds of equity financing completed on December 19, 2025.
  • The company reported a net loss before taxes of $4.3 million for the quarter, compared to $1.1 million in the prior-year period. 
  • For the three-month period ended November 30, 2025, the company recorded an EBITDA loss of $2.3 million compared to $2 million in the prior-year period.
  • VMAR -2.428% premarket to $0.1447.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SEALSQ announces preliminary 2025 revenue and 2026 outlook
data.description:
• SEALSQ (LAES) said on Tuesday it posted preliminary unaudited revenue of $18 million for 2025, up 66% year on year, supported by renewed demand for legacy products and the consolidation of revenue from IC’ALPS following its August acquisition.

  • The semiconductor company said it recorded $8 million of revenue in the fourth quarter and ended the year with more than $425 million in cash, providing liquidity to support expansion across its quantum-security ecosystem.

  • SEALSQ reiterated its forecast for 2026 revenue growth of between 50% and 100% year on year, citing a full year of IC’ALPS revenue, new post-quantum chip launches and initial revenue from the Quantix Edge project.
  • Concensus for 2025 revenue is $17.95M.
  • The company said its business pipeline exceeds $200 million in potential opportunities from 2026 to 2028.
  • LAES +0.68% premarket to $4.38.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Equinor awarded 35 new production licenses on Norwegian continental shelf
data.description:
Equinor (EQNR) said Tuesday it was awarded 35 new production licenses by Norway's Ministry of Energy in this year's awards in predefined areas, part of the government's annual licensing round for the best-known exploration areas on the Norwegian shelf.

The annual APA rounds of new offshore exploration acreage are central to Norway's strategy of extending oil and gas production for several more decades, although activity is set to decline in the coming years.

The company said 21 of the awards are located in the North Sea, 10 in the Norwegian Sea, and four in the Barents Sea, including 17 of the licences with Equinor (EQNR) as an operator.

Equinor (EQNR) said discovery and maturation of new resources are necessary for it to develop 6-8 new subsea developments each year until 2035, a significant increase from current levels.

"Access to new acreage is crucial for our ambition to maintain a high level of production and predictable energy deliveries to Europe from the [Norwegian continental shelf] towards 2035. There is still a lot of energy left on the NCS, but we need new discoveries to curb the expected production decline," Equinor (EQNR) Senior VP Jez Averty said.

data.title: Rezolve Ai raises 2025–2026 revenue outlook, forecasts $350M in 2026 sales
data.description:
• Rezolve Ai (RZLV) issued updated revenue guidance, projecting at least $40M in full-year 2025 revenue, vs analyst expectations of $40.2M.

  • For full-year 2026, the company forecasts ~$350M in revenue, nearly double market consensus and representing nearly 10× year-over-year growth.

  • Rezolve reaffirmed its expectation to exit 2026 with a minimum $500M in annual recurring revenue, implying roughly $40M in monthly revenue by December 2026.

  • The company exited 2025 with ~$209M in ARR.

  • Rezolve reported a record December with expected revenue above $17M, marking its first profitable month.

  • RZLV shares up 7% premarket.

data.title: CPI read: Food prices outpace general inflation due to beef and coffee pressures
data.description:
The Consumer Price Index report for December indicated a 2.6% year-over-year increase for the core rate, which fell slightly below the expectation of economists and was level with the pace seen in November.

The closely watched food category was up 3.1% year-over-year in December, with food away from home up 4.1% Y/Y during the month and food at home up 2.4% Y/Y.

The food category with the biggest jump in prices compared to a year ago was once again beef. Uncooked ground beef (+15.5%), uncooked beef roasts (+17.5%), and uncooked beef steaks (17.8%) all showed elevated prices. The overall meats category was up 9.2% in December.

Beef prices in the U.S. are currently near record levels due to a combination of supply shortages driven by long-term droughts, high feed costs, reduced cattle inventories, and strong consumer demand for beef. Notably, the national cattle herd is at its lowest level in decades after severe droughts over the last five years forced ranchers to reduce breeding herds as grazing lands and feed became scarce. The replenishment cycle is expected to take several years. In addition, analysts note that tariffs on imports and trade disruptions have limited foreign supply, which has also contributed to higher beef prices in the U.S.

A broad list of companies impacted by beef prices includes JBS N.V. (JBS), Kroger (KR), Albertsons (ACI), Walmart (WMT), Tyson Foods (TSN), Hormel (HRL), Pilgrim's Pride (PPC), Kraft Heinz (KHC), Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Texas Roadhouse (TXRH), Shake Shack (SHAK), McDonald's (MCD), Wendy's (WEN), Restaurant Brands International (QSR), and Beyond Meat (BYND).

Coffee prices also showed a big jump, up 19.8% from a year ago in December. There was tariff relief for the sector in November, but it is unclear when prices will fall at the retail level. Companies potentially impacted by the level of coffee prices include Starbucks Corporation (SBUX), Dutch Bros (BROS), First Watch Restaurant Group (FWRG), Coffee Holding (JVA), Dunkin' Brands, J.M. Smucker (SJM), Starbucks (SBUX), Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB), McDonald's (MCD), Costa Coffee (KO), Tim Hortons (QSR), Luckin Coffee (LKNCY), Nestle (NSRGY), Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), Kraft Heinz (KHC), and Krispy Kreme (DNUT).

data.title: NetraMark announces private placement financing
data.description:
• NetraMark (AINMF) announced on Tuesday a non-brokered private placement of up to 3.5M units of the company at $1.00 per unit, for aggregate gross proceeds of up to C$3.5M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the Company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each whole warrant will entitle the holder to acquire one additional common share in the capital of the company at an exercise price of $1.35 per share for a period of two years from the closing date of the offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general working capital and corporate purposes, including continued advancement and commercialization of its proprietary NetraAI platform and support of ongoing and prospective biopharmaceutical engagements.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Concentrix declares $0.36 dividend
data.description:
• Concentrix (CNXC) declares $0.36/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.56%
  • Payable Feb. 10; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.
  • See CNXC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Mid to low cap energy stocks with the weakest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off this week, we look at mid-to-low market cap energy stocks with weak earnings momentum.

These stocks face the most challenging analyst outlooks, spanning various subsectors including oil and gas exploration and production, equipment and services, storage and transportation, and refining and marketing.

Each stock on this list has received an ‘F’ grade for EPS revisions, the lowest possible rating. This grade indicates that analysts have made significant downward revisions to their earnings estimates for these companies.

Here are the mid-low cap energy stocks with the worst EPS revision grades.

  1. Advantage Energy (AAVVF) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  2. Forum Energy Technologies (FET) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  3. Genesis Energy, L.P.  (GEL) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  4. Golar LNG (GLNG) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  5. GeoPark (GPRK) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  6. HighPeak Energy (HPK) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  7. Kodiak Gas Services (KGS) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  8. Kinetik Holdings (KNTK) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  9. Oil States International (OIS) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  10. OPAL Fuels (OPAL) — EPS Revision Grade: F

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Merck signals appetite for multi-billion-dollar acquisitions
data.description:
• Pharmaceutical giant Merck & Co (MRK) said it is looking to strike more deals, pointing to opportunities for acquisitions worth “multi tens of billions” of dollars.
  • “We are not limited from a balance sheet — it’s more where do we see strategic opportunity,” Chief Executive Officer Rob Davis said at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference in San Francisco.
  • Davis said the company’s progress to date has given it visibility into more than $70 billion in commercial opportunities through the mid-2030s.
  • The WSJ reported on Friday that Merck was in discussions to acquire the cancer-drug biotech for about $30 billion. Revolution Medicines (RVMD) however, declined to comment on recent M&A speculation.

data.title: Core CPI inflation comes in slightly cooler than expected in December
data.description:
December Consumer Price Index: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Tuesday.

+2.7% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.7% prior.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy): +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior.

+2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

Developing... check back for updates.
